scope: This audit classifies source compatibility. It does not reprove the cited papers or establish a factorial divisor-density theorem.

sources.legal_use: background comparison and warning against average-to-pointwise transfer
sources.quantifier_scope: ambient count over all positive integers m<=x
sources.title: The distribution of integers with a divisor in a given interval

sources.legal_use: methodological compatibility with independent exponent coordinates only
sources.quantifier_scope: almost all y-friable integers in S(x,y), with an explicit exceptional set
sources.source_object: Gaussian divisor law for all but a quantified exceptional subset of S(x,y)
sources.title: Lois de repartition des diviseurs des entiers friables

sources.legal_use: source-scope comparison only; no lower-bound transfer to exact factorial divisors
sources.quantifier_scope: arithmetic-progression distribution in the common-cap ultrafriable set U(x,y)
sources.source_object: U(x,y), integers m<=x with every occurring prime power <=y
sources.title: Entiers ultrafriables en progressions arithmetiques

drappeau_tenenbaum_exceptional_scope.explanation: Membership in S(x,y) does not prove that this deterministic member lies outside the theorem's exceptional subset.

ford_ambient_vs_fixed.explanation: The ambient count is positive while the fixed factorial has no divisor in the interval.

ultrafriable_common_cap_mismatch.case_y_at_least_required_power: 11 is included in U(10!,y) although 11 does not divide 10!
ultrafriable_common_cap_mismatch.explanation: No common ultrafriability parameter y makes U(10!,y) equal the divisor set of 10!.
